titel: Expanded criminal liability for attempted crimes, preparation for crimes, and incitement to crimes.
html:
Organized crime poses a threat to the entire system and must be prevented and combated with the full capacity of society. To be able to intervene at an early stage and prevent serious crimes from being completed, the government proposes to expand criminal liability for attempted crimes, preparation, and incitement.

The expanded liability for attempted crimes is proposed to take effect on April 1, 2026. The other proposed amendments to the law are proposed to take effect on July 1, 2026.

Proposal for a Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2025/26:49

Government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Criminal Code.
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Criminal Code.

3.

Prop. 2025/26:49 2
tldr: The government wants to make it easier to punish individuals who plan or attempt to commit serious crimes. The aim is to be able to stop gang-related crime before the crimes actually occur.
lattlast:
The government has proposed a plan to combat organized crime. They want to amend the law so that individuals who prepare or attempt to commit crimes can face harsher penalties. This is intended to help the police and society intervene earlier.

The proposal involves expanding the concept of criminal liability. This means that individuals can be punished even if they have only prepared a crime or attempted to carry it out, without the crime actually being successful. This is part of the effort to stop crime that threatens the entire society.

If the proposal is approved, the new rules regarding attempted crimes will come into effect on April 1, 2026. The other proposed changes to the law are scheduled to take effect on July 1, 2026. Now, the Swedish Parliament, which is Sweden's legislative body, will vote on the proposal.
